questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the Nawabi foundation of Awadh, the title 'Burhan-ul-Mulk' is associated with which of the following figures?
questions.explanation: Saadat Khan, the Persian adventurer who was appointed Nawab of Awadh in 1722, was also known by the title Burhan-ul-Mulk.
questions.takeaway: Burhan-ul-Mulk is the title of the founder of the Nawabi of Awadh, Saadat Khan.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Persian adventurer Saadat Khan, also known as Burhan-ul-Mulk, was appointed Nawab of Awadh in 1722.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The Battle of Buxar and the subsequent Treaty of Allahabad (1765) represented a structural shift in the governance of Awadh by:

options.text: Ending the hereditary rule of the Nawabs and establishing direct British Crown rule.
Transitioning the state from a period of independent Nawabi authority to one of Company-led administrative oversight.
Relocating the administrative capital from Lucknow back to Faizabad to ensure military security.
Formally reintegrating Awadh as a direct province of the Mughal Empire to counter the East India Company.

questions.explanation: The Battle of Buxar resulted in the defeat of the combined forces, and the Treaty of Allahabad (1765) forced Awadh under the administration of the East India Company. This marked the beginning of significant British influence over the state's internal and external affairs, ending its brief period of relative independence.
questions.takeaway: The 1765 Treaty of Allahabad was the mechanism through which the British East India Company established its first formal administrative grip over the Nawabi state of Awadh.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Battle of Buxar... leading to Awadh's defeat. Treaty of Allahabad Signed in 1765, forcing Awadh under Company administration. British Control Marked the beginning of British influence over Awadh's affairs.
